title: Trump seeks deal to neutralize Iran's highly enriched uranium stockpile
subhed: The US president claims he can secure a better deal than the 2015 agreement signed by Barack Obama, but Tehran is refusing to grant Washington even a symbolic victory on the issue.

title: Heatwave in France: 'This is an unprecedented event, with about a one-in-1,000 chance of occurring in a given year'

Drone racing to relax: A weekend away from the front with Ukrainian soldiers
subhed: Arms manufacturers and soldiers combine business with pleasure at a drone-racing competition where they share their frontline experience. A new warrior subculture is emerging, signaling Ukraine's long-term militarization.

title: French Open: Out-of-form Loïs Boisson returns to stage of her breakthrough
subhed: The top-ranked French player and a surprise semifinalist in 2025 spent seven months off the court due to a series of physical issues. Still searching for her best tennis, she faces Russia's Anna Kalinskaya in the first round on Tuesday.

title: Sonny Rollins, giant of jazz and the saxophone, has died
subhed: The creator of some 20 albums that are landmarks in the history of jazz, the former collaborator of John Coltrane, Charlie Parker and Miles Davis pioneered a unique form of marathon concert. He died on May 25 at the age of 95.

title: How China uses influencers to polish its image
subhed: Chongqing, a futuristic Chinese city, has caught the eye of influencers and bloggers. Beijing, meanwhile, welcomes new visitors, often unaware of the geopolitical and ideological currents running beneath their social media posts.

The post-May '68 Christian philosophy now being embraced by the American far right
subhed: Trump supporters often blame the cultural upheavals triggered by the May 1968 protests in France for sowing the seeds of 'wokeness.' Yet some of them are now claiming a different French school of thought, which took shape at Stanford University under the influence of René Girard.

title: Why dropping the worst-case climate scenario does not undermine the reality of global warming
subhed: A new generation of climate scenarios has set aside RCP8.5, which projected a nearly 5°C rise in global temperatures by 2100. Donald Trump presents this as proof of 'alarmism' among climate scientists, but the new projections remain highly concerning.

title: France's film funding agency targeted by the far right
subhed: Without the National Center for Cinema and the Moving Image, many French films could never be made. But the CNC is now under attack, which could spell the end of this nearly 80-year-old institution if the far-right Rassemblement National comes to power.
